attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.
Median home value in a block group divided by median household income. HUD defines affordability as a rate less than 3.36.
attr.attrdefs: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates Table B19013, B25077

attr.attrdef: The number of students who met or exceeded the set testing standard in 3rd grade reading and 7th grade math testing divided by the total students within the block group.
attr.attrdefs: NCES School Locations, Washington State Report Card

attr.attrdef: The aggregate frequency of public transit service (trips per hour) in each census block group.
The number of people 18 or older who have ever been diagnosed with cancer or melanoma by a health professional divided by the total population of 18 or older for the area.
The number of students that miss 10% or more of the school days, (missing more than 2 days a month on average) divided by the total number of students.
attr.attrdefs: NCES School Locations, Washington State Report Card

attr.attrdef: Number of commuters (workers who don’t work from home) who commute fewer than 15 minutes to work divided by all commuters.
attr.attrdefs: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates Table B08012

attr.attrdef: Number of owners and renters paying more than 30% of income on monthly housing cost expenses divided by total households.
attr.attrdefs: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates Table B25091, B25070

attr.attrdef: Area of the block group within 2 miles of a Community or Public Recreation Center divided by the total area of the block group.

Count of personal and property crimes within the past two years (October 2022 to October 2024), including offenses such assault, rape, murder, theft, vehicle theft, and vandalism divided by total population for the block group and then multiplied 1,000.
Area of block group within one mile of a daycare in urban areas and two miles of a daycare in rural areas divided by the total area of the block group.
attr.attrdefs: DCYF License Data via Washington State Open Data

attr.attrdef: The number of people 18 or older who have ever been diagnosed as having a depressive disorder by a health professional divided by the total population of 18 or older for the area.
Annual average particulate matter from diesel engine emissions (micrograms per cubic meter), estimated by EPA modeling. For more information, see methodology on EJ Screen
Summed the violations (of the Safe Drinking Water Act) that were reported for the various water systems and allocated the value to the respective block groups within those water systems.
attr.attrdefs: EPA water system boundaries, water system violation report Q3 2024

attr.attrdef: The number of High school students who completed a dual credit course divided by the total high school students (Includes Running Start, CTE Dual Credit (formerly Tech Prep), College in High School, Advanced Placement, International Baccalaureate, and Cambridge International).
attr.attrdefs: NCES School Locations, Washington State Report Card

attr.attrdef: Weighted average index educational attainment in total population over 25. Educational attainment index = (1 * Associates Degree) + (1.2 * Bachelor’s Degree) + (1.5 * Master’s or PhD Degree).
Average number of days over 90F (from 2021-2024) per block group. This is calculated from PRISM Climate Group’s gridded daily Tmax estimates.
The combined wildfire likelihood and intensity within the region as a simulated model
attr.attrdefs: U.S Department of Agriculture Wildfire Risk to Communities

attr.attrdef: The total acres of the FEMA-designated flood zone within a block group divided by the total acres of the block group.
Area of a block group within 1 mile of an urban Women, Infants and Children (WIC) Nutrition Program retailer or 2 miles of a rural one divided by the total area of the block group.

The 4-year adjusted cohort graduation rate. This is the number of students who completed high school in four years, divided by the number of students who are in their "cohort": those that started 9th grade at the same time. "Adjusted" refers how some students may have moved in or out of the school, and the cohort size needs to be adjusted to accurately track those students.
attr.attrdefs: NCES School Locations, Washington State Report Card

attr.attrdef: The number of people without healthcare insurance divided by the civilian, non-institutionalized population.
attr.attrdefs: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates Table B27010

attr.attrdef: Average count of vehicles per day on a roadway divided by distance of the roadway from the center of the block group.
Number jobs within the block group that have monthly wages of $3,333 or greater divided by the total jobs in the block group.
Number of homeowners (owner-occupied housing-units) divided by total households.
attr.attrdefs: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates Table B25003

attr.attrdef: Total households with internet access divided by all households.
attr.attrdefs: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates Table B28002

attr.attrdef: The number of kindergarten students that demonstrate readiness in 5 out of 6 areas of development and learning (social-emotional, physical, language, cognitive, literacy, and math), divided by the total number of kindergarten students.
attr.attrdefs: NCES School Locations, Washington State Report Card

attr.attrdef: Area of block group within one mile of a library in urban areas and two miles of a library in rural areas divided by the total area of the block group.

The median household income in a given area. It includes the gross, pretax cash income of the householder and all other people 15 years old and older in the household, regardless of relation to the householder.
attr.attrdefs: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates Table B19013

attr.attrdef: Count of overdose calls within a block group from 2022 to 2024 divided by the population and multiplied by 1,000
Annual mean of the 10 highest recorded ground-level ozone concentration days (parts per billion), as estimated by EPA. For more information, see methodology on EJ Screen.
Area of the block group within 2 miles of a Parks and Open Space divided by the total area of the block group.
The percentage difference in the life expectancy of the area to the maximum life expectancy for the nation.
The area of the block group that is covered by tree canopy divided by the total area of the block group.

Particulate Matter (PM) 2.5 measures potential exposure to tiny air particles that can be inhaled (2.5 microns or smaller per cubic meter in a block group), with levels reported as annual averages. For more information, see methodology on EJ Screen.
The number of people 18 years or older who report having "poor" or "fair" overall health divided by the total population of 18 or older for the area.
Number of individuals within block group earning less than 200% of the Federal Poverty Level divided by the total population for which poverty is calculated.
attr.attrdefs: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates Table C17002

attr.attrdef: The student-to-teacher ratio is the number of students in the organization divided by the number of teachers.
attr.attrdefs: NCES School Locations, Washington State Report Card

attr.attrdef: Total count of proposed or listed superfund sites (locations that have contaminated hazardous waste) divided by distance to the block group. For more information, see methodology on EJ Screen.

Transit access is based on three different areas: (1) acreage of a block group's area within 1/4 mile of rail and bus service stops, (2) acreage within 3 miles of park and ride locations, and (3) acreage of micro transit service areas. Each of the three acreages are summed together and divided by the acreage of the block group. Overlapping transit areas yield higher totals, meaning greater transit access.
attr.attrdefs: Sound Transit, Pierce County Transit, Park & Ride locations

attr.attrdef: The number of people who are unemployed divided by the workforce over 16 years of age. In order to be unemployed, the individual has to be seeking a job (people not able to work or not seeking work are not included in this estimate).
attr.attrdefs: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates Table B23025

attr.attrdef: Number of households that have at least one vehicle divided by number of households in the block group.
attr.attrdefs: American Community Survey 5-Year Estimates Table B25044

attr.attrdef: The number of voters within a block group who returned a ballot in the general election is divided by the number of registered voters in the block group. The voter participation data included reflect only turnout during general presidential elections and do not include primaries or other election types

A weighted estimate of the degree of pedestrian-oriented street connectivity for each census block group, measured as street intersections per square mile (specifically, three or more legs). Intersections that exclude pedestrian passage were not counted.
Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.
Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.

attr.attalias: Ave Transit Stop Per Hour zScore
attr.attrdef: Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.
Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.
Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.
Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.
Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.

attr.attalias: Community and Rec Center Access zScore
attr.attrdef: Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.
Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.
Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.

Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.
Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.
Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.

Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.

Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.
Each area is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for the specific indicator.
Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Area of feature in internal units squared.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Community impact index measures the average score of all 6 categories, providing a single assessment of community conditions for Pierce County.

dataIdInfo.idPurp: The Pierce County Community Impact Index data highlights opportunities to improve equitable access and outcomes for residents of Pierce County. This Index includes an overall Community Impact Index rating which is made up of six categories (Community Vitality, Education, Environmental Health, Health and Wellness, Housing and Infrastructure, Jobs and Economy), and 42 individual data points. The data is presented in the Pierce County Community Impact Index web application (https://piercecountywa.communityimpactindex.econw.io).
dataIdInfo.idAbs:
The Pierce County Community Impact Index data highlights opportunities to improve equitable access and outcomes for residents of Pierce County. The very low definition is indicative of overall lower community conditions and vice versa. This Index includes an overall Community Impact Index rating which is made up of six categories (Community Vitality, Education, Environmental Health, Health and Wellness, Housing and Infrastructure, Jobs and Economy), and 42 individual data points. The data is presented in the Pierce County Community Impact Index web application (https://piercecountywa.communityimpactindex.econw.io).

How Are Index Scores Calculated?

Each block group is assigned a z-score, which indicates how it compares to the county average for a specific indicator. After normalizing the underlying estimates, these z-scores are averaged across all indicators within a category to calculate the Category Score. Finally, the Category Scores are averaged to produce the Overall Community Impact Index Score.

The z-scores range from negative to positive values, where:

  • Negative Scores: Indicate performance below the county average.

  • Scores of 0 Indicate performance at the county average.

  • Positive Scores: Indicate performance above the county average.

These scores help you evaluate the relative strengths and weaknesses of block groups in terms of community dimensions such as housing, health, and education. They also provide insights into how these dimensions contribute to the overall community impact index.

Community Impact Index: measures the average score of all 6 categories, providing a single assessment of equity for Pierce County.

Community Vitality: Measures community participation and connection, including access to amenities and services that contribute to the overall well-being of a community. Indicators include, Community and Recreation Center Access, Crime Rate, Daycare Access, Heavy Traffic Proximity, Library Access, Park Access, Transit Access, Voter Participation.

Education: Measures educational outcomes. Indicators include, Assessment met Standard, Chronic Absentee, Dual Credit Enrollment, Education Attainment, High School Graduation Rate, Kindergarten Readiness, Student Teacher Ratio.

Environmental Health: Measures environmental factors that can impact community safety and health. Indicators include, Diesel Emissions, Drinking Water Non-Compliant, Extreme Heat Days, Fire Risk, Flood Risk, Ozone Concentration, Tree Canopy Cover, Particulate Matter (PM) 2.5 Concentration, Superfund Site Proximity.

Health and Wellness: Measures health results and access to healthy living. Indicators include: Cancer Rate, Depression Prevalence, Healthy Food Access, Health Uninsured rate, Opiod Overdose Call Rate, Low Life Expectancy, Poor Health.

Housing and Infrastructure: Measures housing conditions and community access to important infrastructure. Indicators include, Housing Affordability, Average Transit Stop Per Hour, Total Cost Burdened Households, Homeownership Rate, Internet Access

Jobs and Economy: Measures the local economy and job access. Indicators include, Commute under 15 min, High Quality Jobs, Median Household Income, Households at 200% of the Poverty Line or Less, Unemployment Rate, Vehicle Access.

Consts.useLimit: No access constraints. These data are available for use by the public. Users are advised to read the metadata thoroughly to understand appropriate use and data limitations.

dataLineage.statement: Data were collected at the block group level where possible. Aggregations are built from block group level data.
